articles.description: Roseburg Parks and Recreation will open its 2026 Arbor Day Photo Contest on March 12, inviting people to submit one photo of a tree or grove of trees taken in the Roseburg area. Entries are due by 5 p.m. April 17, with winners to be announced on Arbor Day, April 24.
articles.title: Roseburg Arbor Day Photo Contest Opens March 12

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: DFPA is hiring for the 2026 fire season, with seasonal openings for wildland firefighters, dispatchers and wildfire detection camera operators. Applications are open now, with reviews starting in April, interviews in May and onboarding in June.
articles.title: Douglas Forest Protective Association Opens Hiring For 2026 Fire Season

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop
articles.title: March 6, 2025

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported a theft arrest at Sherms, multiple prohibited-camping lodgings near Deer Creek, a trespass/harassment arrest after a disturbance, a vehicle vs. pedestrian crash near Roseburg Christian Academy, a public drinking citation, and a DUII arrest.
articles.title: Police Log: March 4, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Bergin College of Canine Studies needs volunteer puppy raisers by next week for four dogs: Trendy (2), Zabin (9 months), Antje (5 months) and Amanda (5 months). The home stage builds the confidence and routines future service dogs need.
articles.title: Bergin College Seeks Volunteer Puppy Raisers for Four Dogs Needing Homes by Next Week to Help Change Lives

articles.description: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop
articles.title: March 5, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg Forest Products restarted construction March 4, 2026 on its Dillard MDF plant, featuring a Siempelkamp continuous press to make MDF and HDF from wood residuals. The company expects 140+ jobs when operations begin in late 2028.
articles.title: Roseburg Forest Products Reboots Dillard MDF Project, Plans 140+ Jobs When Plant Opens

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported arrests for warrants (including meth found at jail), a DUII-related crash on SE Pine, and a criminal prohibited-camping citation near NE Garden Valley, according to the city’s daily police log.
articles.title: Police Log: March 3, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Earth Day and Energy Fair 2026 is set for Saturday, April 18, 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. on SE Main Street in downtown Roseburg. The free public event will feature exhibits, info tables, kids activities, and sessions on energy, gardening, native plants and more.
articles.title: Earth Day and Energy Fair 2026 Planned in Downtown Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop
articles.title: March 4, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A free public screening of the documentary “HERE. IS. BETTER.” will be held Saturday, March 21, 2026, at noon at the Roseburg Public Library. The event includes a panel discussion moderated by Returning Veterans Project Executive Director Amy Almond-Schmid. Admission is free.
articles.title: Free Veteran Mental Health Documentary Screening Set For March 21 In Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Body of a man who went under in the South Umpqua River near Portland Avenue on Feb. 27 was recovered March 2 by the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office Dive Team. He was identified as 46-year-old Johnathon Michael Laine of Roseburg. The case remains under investigation as a boating incident.
articles.title: Body Recovered, Man Identified After South Umpqua River Drowning

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg will flush fire hydrants citywide April 1 to June 30, 2026, weekdays from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Temporary low pressure or discolored water may occur. Water is safe to drink, but run it until clear before washing clothes or dishes.
articles.title: Hydrant Flushing Scheduled Across Roseburg City Limits April 1 Through June 30

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Firefighters extinguished a structure fire in a single-wide home on Apricot Lane at Evergreen Village Mobile Home Park in Green within about 10 minutes of arrival. Central Douglas Fire & Rescue said the cause was accidental, likely improper cigarette disposal.
articles.title: Accidental Cigarette Disposal Blamed For Green Mobile Home Fire

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police arrested a 30-year-old man after a stolen vehicle linked to a robbery failed to yield during a stop attempt. Spike strips were deployed near NE Stephens and NE Garden Valley, and the vehicle stopped in the roadway. He was lodged at Corrections.
articles.title: Attempt To Elude Arrest Made After Stolen Vehicle Stop Attempt In Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg Police Department made multiple arrests and citations, including a stolen-vehicle elude arrest, warrant and trespass arrests, a probation-violation detainer, and DUII arrests tied to suspected impairment and drug possession.
articles.title: Police Log: March 2, 2026

articles.description: A new $2.5 million outdoor tennis and pickleball facility is set to open this week in Roseburg, according to city officials.
articles.title: Roseburg opens $2.5M tennis and pickleball courts in Stewart Park
